description: A bronchodilator for asthma and COPD, in use since the 1930s, and the dimethylxanthine isomer of theobromine. Its useful blood levels sit right on the bend of its own saturation curve, so the body clears it more slowly as the dose rises and levels climb more than proportionally - which is why it is dosed against measured blood levels.

vd.conditions: Derived from Lelo 1986's directly-measured theophylline disposition (theophylline dosed on its own in six healthy males): plasma clearance 0.93 mL/min/kg and half-life 6.2 h give Vd = CL.t-half/ln2 = 0.0558 L/h/kg / 0.1118 /h = 0.499 L/kg. Deliberately BYTE-IDENTICAL to the volume caffeine.json stores for its theophylline metabolite line, so the two files cannot disagree about the same molecule (see notes). Independently corroborated by the FDA label's 'approximately 0.45 L/kg (range 0.3-0.7 L/kg) based on ideal body weight'. This is the ONE parameter here NOT taken from the Vmax/Km source - permissible, and argued in the notes, because this compound's payload is Vd-free. Scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time.

vmax.conditions: pooled maximum elimination rate across 10 normal subjects (Wagner 1985), estimated by fitting the Michaelis-Menten relation to reported single-dose clearances and the lower steady-state clearances of the same literature subjects - i.e. Vmax and Km are read out of the very fact that clearance FALLS as exposure rises. This is a LUMPED whole-body Vmax, not an enzyme constant: theophylline actually runs three parallel capacity-limited pathways plus ~10% first-order renal excretion (see notes). Reported as a rate for a normal adult and used unscaled against the 70 kg reference subject. It is the least certain number in this compound and it is the one that sets the dose-to-Css cliff - see the notes for Dahlqvist 1984's competing 2642 mg/day (611 micromol/h in 11 healthy volunteers) and why Wagner's is kept.

km.conditions: total serum theophylline concentration at which elimination runs at half of Vmax (Wagner 1985, same 10-subject pooled fit as Vmax - the two are correlated estimates and are taken together). It sits just ABOVE the 10-15 mg/L therapeutic range, which is the whole point of this compound: theophylline is used exactly where its enzymes are approaching half-saturation, on the bend of the curve rather than on either straight part. Corroborated to within 0.6% by the only other independent overall-elimination fit: Dahlqvist 1984's 133 micromol/L is 23.96 mg/L (x 180.16 g/mol). The two studies nonetheless disagree by 35% on Vmax, which is the parameter that actually sets the cliff (see notes).

metabolism: About 90% of a theophylline dose is metabolised in the liver and roughly 10% is excreted unchanged in the urine. The FDA label states the crucial fact plainly: 'Both the N-demethylation and hydroxylation pathways of theophylline biotransformation are capacity-limited.' Demethylation to 3-methylxanthine is catalysed by CYP1A2; CYP2E1 and CYP3A3 hydroxylate it to 1,3-dimethyluric acid. Because those enzyme pathways saturate, theophylline does not clear a constant FRACTION of what is present per hour - the property that gives ordinary drugs a fixed half-life. Instead its clearance falls as its concentration rises, so serum levels rise faster than dose. The label draws the clinical conclusion directly: 'Since this non-linearity results in more than proportional changes in serum theophylline concentrations with changes in dose, it is advisable to make increases or decreases in dose in small increments.' Layered on top of the saturation is a very large between-person spread: clearance rises about 50% in young adult tobacco smokers and about 80% in elderly smokers, and falls about 40% after one week of abstinence. No metabolite line is drawn: the three products (3-methylxanthine, 1-methyluric acid, 1,3-dimethyluric acid) are each formed by their OWN saturable pathway, which the metabolite engine - built for first-order formation off a linear parent - cannot express.
displayNote: Theophylline has no single half-life. The curve is drawn from Vmax and Km, so the apparent half-life changes with concentration - that is the drug, not an approximation. The same molecule is ALSO drawn as a linear metabolite line on caffeine, where it sits ~100-300x below the Km that bends this curve.

fda_label.title: THEOPHYLLINE - FDA Structured Product Label, CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY ('Both the N-demethylation and hydroxylation pathways of theophylline biotransformation are capacity-limited.'; 'Due to the wide intersubject variability of the rate of theophylline metabolism, non-linearity of elimination may begin in some patients at serum theophylline concentrations <10 mcg/mL. Since this non-linearity results in more than proportional changes in serum theophylline concentrations with changes in dose, it is advisable to make increases or decreases in dose in small increments'; 'In general, maintaining peak serum theophylline concentrations between 10 and 15 mcg/mL will achieve most of the drug's potential therapeutic benefit while minimizing the risk of serious adverse events.'; apparent volume of distribution approximately 0.45 L/kg, range 0.3-0.7 L/kg, based on ideal body weight; mean clearance in otherwise healthy non-smoking adult asthmatics 16-60 years 0.65 mL/kg/min, range 0.27-1.03; approximately 90% of the dose metabolised in the liver, approximately 10% excreted unchanged in the urine; demethylation to 3-methylxanthine catalysed by CYP1A2, hydroxylation to 1,3-dimethyluric acid by CYP2E1 and CYP3A3; clearance increased ~50% in young adult tobacco smokers and ~80% in elderly smokers, reduced ~40% after one week of abstinence)

wagner.title: Wagner JG. Theophylline. Pooled Michaelis-Menten parameters (Vmax and Km) and implications. Clin Pharmacokinet. 1985 Sep-Oct;10(5):432-42 (PMID 3899457). Pooled Michaelis-Menten parameters estimated for 10 normal subjects from reported single-dose clearances and the lower steady-state clearances of theophylline: mean Vmax 1960 mg/day, mean Km 24.1 mg/L, for OVERALL theophylline elimination.

tang_liu.title: Tang-Liu DD, Williams RL, Riegelman S. Nonlinear theophylline elimination. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 1982 Mar;31(3):358-69 (PMID 7060318). Elimination kinetics of theophylline and its major metabolites in 14 healthy adults, single-dose and multiple-plateau studies, plasma concentrations followed down to ~0.020 mg/L: 'Metabolite formation of the three metabolites followed Michaelis-Menten kinetics and became capacity limited within the therapeutic range of theophylline.' Approximate mean Vmax for formation of 3-methylxanthine, 1-methyluric acid and 1,3-dimethyluric acid: 5, 13 and 34 mg/h; apparent Km for those pathways: 2.7, 9.3 and 14.2 mg/L.

dahlqvist.title: Dahlqvist R, Billing B, Miners JO, Birkett DJ. Nonlinear metabolic disposition of theophylline. Ther Drug Monit. 1984;6(3):290-7 (PMID 6506136). Eleven healthy volunteers; the Km for OVERALL theophylline elimination averaged 133 micromol/L (range 55-213 micromol/L) and the Vmax 611 micromol/h (2,640 mg/day; range 452-813 micromol/h). 'Considerable interindividual variability in these parameters and particularly Km:s was found.' The second independent overall-elimination fit, and the competing Vmax estimate discussed in the notes; Miners and Birkett are also authors of the Lelo 1986 papers this compound takes its Vd from.

gundert_remy.title: Gundert-Remy U, Hildebrandt R, Hengen N, Weber E. Non-linear elimination processes of theophylline. Eur J Clin Pharmacol. 1983;24(1):71-8 (PMID 6832205). Four healthy subjects; total clearance 65.5 +/- 11.3 mL/min, which DECREASED as dose increased - an independent direct demonstration of the nonlinearity.

lelo_pk.title: Lelo A, Birkett DJ, Robson RA, Miners JO. Comparative pharmacokinetics of caffeine and its primary demethylated metabolites paraxanthine, theobromine and theophylline in man. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 1986;22(2):177-182 (PMID 3756065). Six healthy male volunteers, each compound given orally on separate occasions: plasma clearances caffeine 2.07, paraxanthine 2.20, theobromine 1.20, theophylline 0.93 mL/min/kg; half-lives caffeine 4.1, paraxanthine 3.1, theobromine 7.2, theophylline 6.2 h.

notes:
The set's THIRD nonlinear compound (after phenytoin and ethanol) and the one that closes a loop the data already contained: theophylline was drawn as a caffeine metabolite months before it could ship on its own, with its own notes explaining why it could not.

WHY IT SHIPS NOW. Theophylline was tagged 'linear: false, excluded' from the start (handoff §12; docs/DATA_GUIDE.md named it and salicylate 'the plausible next MM ships'). The exclusion was never about principle - it was that nobody had curated a Vmax and Km. Wagner 1985 supplies both, and the Michaelis-Menten engine (engine/modelsMM.ts) integrates the schedule as an ODE instead of superposing.

THE TEACHING PAYLOAD - a gentler, commoner nonlinearity than phenytoin, and an isomer pair. Two things make this compound worth a slot even though phenytoin already teaches saturation. (1) SCALE. Phenytoin's Km (7.9 mg/L) sits BELOW its therapeutic range, so it is already past half-saturation in normal use and its cliff is violent (a 33% dose rise more than doubles Css). Theophylline's Km (24.1 mg/L) sits just ABOVE its 10-15 mg/L range: it is used ON the bend, not past it. Doubling the maintenance rate from 400 to 800 mg/day moves Css from 6.2 to 16.6 mg/L - 2.7-fold, not 2-fold, and it crosses the whole therapeutic window. That is a nonlinearity you must respect but can still dose against, which is the more common clinical shape and a softer first lesson than phenytoin's cliff. (2) THE ISOMER PAIR. Theobromine (compounds/theobromine.json) ships as a clean LINEAR one-compartment compound. Theobromine and theophylline are both dimethylxanthines - same formula, same mass, differing only in which two of three nitrogens carry the methyl groups - and they fall on OPPOSITE sides of the superposition line. Nothing else in the set puts the linear/nonlinear boundary so close together.

THE SAME MOLECULE, TWO REPRESENTATIONS - and why that is not a contradiction. caffeine.json draws theophylline as one of caffeine's three parallel demethylated metabolites, with a fixed FIRST-ORDER half-life of 6.2 h. This file draws it as a saturable standalone with no half-life at all. Both are correct, and the reason is a hundredfold of concentration. As a ~3.4% (mass-fm) metabolite of an ordinary 200 mg caffeine dose, theophylline peaks around 0.08-0.2 mg/L. Km here is 24.1 mg/L - roughly 100-300x higher. At c << Km the Michaelis-Menten rate Vmax.c/(Km+c) collapses to (Vmax/Km).c, an ordinary first-order process; the engine's own 'Km >> C collapse onto models.ts' test pins exactly this limit. So the linear metabolite line is the correct low-concentration limit OF this saturable model, not a rival claim about it. The two files are held together deliberately: the Vd here (0.5 L/kg, lelo_pk) is byte-identical to caffeine.json's, so the two can never disagree about theophylline's volume - the same device that keeps theobromine's standalone and metabolite appearances consistent. Nothing in CI checks this: loader.test.ts derives each file's own routes and never compares theophylline-in-caffeine against theophylline-standalone. It is a prose-and-discipline guarantee.

HOW THE DILUTE LIMIT LINES UP - stated as a residual, not as agreement. The check that matters is whether this saturable model, taken down to zero concentration, reproduces the directly-measured linear theophylline the other file stores. Dilute-limit half-life = ln2.Km.Vd/Vmax = 0.693 x 24.1 x 35 / 81.67 = 7.2 h, against Lelo's directly measured 6.2 h (range 5-9 h). It lands INSIDE Lelo's own range, but it is ~15% high and that residue is real, not rounding. Equivalently, the dilute-limit clearance Vmax/Km = 3.39 L/h = 0.81 mL/kg/min runs ~13% BELOW two independent direct measurements: Lelo's 0.93 mL/kg/min and Gundert-Remy's 65.5 mL/min (0.94 mL/kg/min). The tempting fix - re-deriving Vmax so that Vmax/Km equals Lelo's clearance - is refused: Vmax and Km are CORRELATED estimates from one fit, and moving one while keeping the other produces a saturation curve nobody measured (the standing rule in docs/DATA_GUIDE.md). The honest reading is that Wagner's pooled subjects cleared theophylline ~15% slower than Lelo's six healthy young males, which is unremarkable for a drug whose clearance the FDA label itself says varies ~50-80% with smoking alone.

WHY THE FOUR CLEARANCES ARE ACTUALLY COHERENT (the check that raised confidence). The FDA label reports mean clearance in otherwise healthy non-smoking adult asthmatics as 0.65 mL/kg/min (range 0.27-1.03). That is an APPARENT clearance measured in patients at therapeutic levels, and under saturation apparent clearance must fall as concentration rises - so it should sit BELOW the dilute-limit ceiling. It does: this model gives 0.81 mL/kg/min at c->0, 0.67 at 5 mg/L (essentially the label's mean), and 0.50-0.57 across the 10-15 mg/L therapeutic range. Lelo's and Gundert-Remy's higher figures are low-dose healthy-volunteer measurements and sit just above the ceiling - and, as the Vmax note below sets out, they sit BELOW the second fit's ceiling (Dahlqvist, 1.09 mL/kg/min), so the two saturable fits bracket the two direct measurements rather than contradicting them. Every one of these numbers falls inside the label's own 0.27-1.03 range, and their ORDERING is what a saturable model predicts. Five sources, one coherent picture, one 15% residual named above.

A SINGLE Vmax/Km IS A LUMPED FIT - the honesty point of this compound. Theophylline does not have one saturable enzyme. Tang-Liu 1982 followed all three products down to 0.020 mg/L and found each pathway saturating separately, with wildly different constants: 3-methylxanthine (Vmax ~5 mg/h, Km ~2.7 mg/L), 1-methyluric acid (~13 mg/h, ~9.3 mg/L), 1,3-dimethyluric acid (~34 mg/h, ~14.2 mg/L) - plus ~10% first-order renal excretion that never saturates at all. A sum of three Michaelis-Menten terms plus a linear term is NOT a Michaelis-Menten term, so the single (Vmax, Km) here is an empirical whole-body fit that reproduces how total clearance falls with exposure WITHOUT claiming to be any enzyme's constant. Wagner published it as exactly that, fitted to observed clearances rather than to a mechanism. This is a real, documented limit of the curve, and it is why Km here (24.1) is larger than every individual pathway's Km: it is a weighted composite, not a measurement of anything. The engine has one Vmax and one Km, which is the right resolution for the lesson (clearance falls with dose) and the wrong resolution for the mechanism (which pathway saturates first).

Vmax IS THE SOFT NUMBER AND IT DRIVES THE CLIFF. There are two independent published fits of theophylline's OVERALL elimination, and their disagreement is instructive. They agree on Km almost exactly - Wagner 24.1 mg/L against Dahlqvist 1984's 133 micromol/L = 23.96 mg/L, a 0.6% difference between a pooled literature analysis and an 11-volunteer study - and they differ by 35% on Vmax: 1960 vs 2642 mg/day. That is not cosmetic. At 800 mg/day the two give a Css of 16.6 and 10.4 mg/L: the same qualitative cliff at materially different heights, which is why the SHAPE is the teaching point and the height carries a ~35% question mark.

Wagner's pair is kept for three reasons. (1) Taking Vmax and Km from ONE fit is the standing rule, and Wagner reports both. (2) It is the pooled multi-subject estimate, derived from the fact that clearance falls between single-dose and steady-state - the phenomenon being taught. (3) Most usefully, the two fits BRACKET the direct measurements rather than one being an outlier: their dilute-limit clearances (Vmax/Km) are 0.81 (Wagner) and 1.09 (Dahlqvist) mL/kg/min, and the two independent directly-measured clearances - Lelo's 0.93 and Gundert-Remy's 0.94 - fall between them. Wagner is the low end of a real spread, not a mistake, and choosing it makes this compound's cliff the CONSERVATIVE of the two available (Dahlqvist's larger Vmax would place Css lower at every rate, and would put the cliff further from the therapeutic range than the label's own warning implies). Note that Dahlqvist shares two authors (Miners, Birkett) with the Lelo papers this file takes its Vd from - the Adelaide group measured both the linear low-concentration disposition and a saturable fit.

MAGNITUDE CHECK (the relation this compound exists to show). Steady state under a constant infusion rate R0 is Css = R0.Km/(Vmax - R0), exact, algebraic, and containing no Vd. With Vmax 1960 mg/day and Km 24.1 mg/L: 400 mg/day gives 6.2 mg/L, 600 gives 10.6, 800 gives 16.6, 900 gives 20.5, and 1000 gives 25.1 mg/L. Doubling 400 to 800 nearly TRIPLES the level and carries it from sub-therapeutic to the top of the window; another 25% reaches frank toxicity. Cross-checked against real practice: a typical adult maintenance infusion is ~0.4 mg/kg/h of theophylline (28 mg/h, 672 mg/day for the reference subject), which this model puts at ~12.6 mg/L - inside the label's stated 10-15 mg/L target. Note the aminophylline conversion when comparing to clinical figures: aminophylline is ~80% theophylline by mass, so a 0.5 mg/kg/h aminophylline order is ~0.4 mg/kg/h of the drug plotted here.

WHY Vd IS THE ONE BORROWED PARAMETER. docs/DATA_GUIDE.md requires Vmax, Km AND Vd from one source, because for a compound whose payload is the zero-order slope (Vmax/Vd - ethanol) a borrowed volume silently moves the one number that must be right. Theophylline is the phenytoin case, not the ethanol case: its payload is Css = R0.Km/(Vmax - R0), which contains no Vd at all. Wagner reports no volume, so Vd comes from Lelo - chosen because it is the number that keeps this file and caffeine.json byte-consistent, and independently corroborated by the label's 0.45 L/kg (range 0.3-0.7). Vd sets only how fast the plateau is approached and how high a loading dose peaks; it cannot corrupt the headline demonstration.

ROUTES - IV only. iv_infusion is the real route and the one that matches the teaching point: intravenous aminophylline is given as a loading dose followed by a constant-rate maintenance infusion, which is literally the R0 in Css = R0.Km/(Vmax - R0). iv_bolus is marked available: false and its curve labeled inferred - an IV theophylline loading dose is infused over 20-30 minutes, never pushed, so the instantaneous-input curve is an idealisation rather than a route the drug is given by (the phenytoin/acyclovir posture).

ORAL PARKED ON SOURCING (a lighter gate than phenytoin's). Oral theophylline is real, common, and essentially completely absorbed - so unlike phenytoin, whose oral omission is a category error, this is simply a missing citation. An oral MM compound needs an EXPLICIT cited ka: there is no ke to invert a Tmax against, and Tmax under saturation is itself dose-dependent, so deriveParamsMM refuses to invert rather than fabricate the dose a Tmax was measured at (contrast ethanol, which ships oral because Rangno 1981 reports a measured ka). No citable ADULT ka was sourced this pass - the one candidate found was a paediatric figure, in a source not opened. If an adult measured ka surfaces, oral drops straight in as data.

NO METABOLITE LINE (a limit, not an oversight). Theophylline's three products are each formed by their OWN saturable pathway (Tang-Liu, above). engine/metabolite.ts builds a metabolite as a first-order formation off a linear parent's shared clearance, which is why the route gate excludes MM parents entirely. Drawing 3-methylxanthine here would require capacity-limited formation - a genuinely different model, not a parameter. Correctly absent.

EDUCATIONAL ILLUSTRATION ONLY - not for dosing. Theophylline is dosed against measured serum concentrations, in a population whose clearance varies several-fold with smoking, age, and illness; nothing here is a dose recommendation.
